strands.title: Task Execution & Polling Pattern
strands.content: Three sequential bash task submissions (bnk191til, b583n243e, and b5mvz85yy) remained in 'running' state across multiple polling cycles. Cycle 67 yields new semantic content: assistant transitions from polling status updates to ANALYSIS mode. A Python measurement script executes successfully (bash task result at turn 200), comparing raw request bytes to projected tensor bytes across a 10-turn baseline experiment.

key_claims.claim: Bash polling tasks transitioned from 'running' to 'ready/completed' state by cycle 67
The measurement script successfully executed and produced compression ratio data

strands.title: Tensor Compression Empirical Results
strands.content: New data: 10-turn baseline experiment (baseline_run1) yields measured compression ratios. Raw request sizes grow from 94KB (turn 1) to 357KB (turn 10). Tensor sizes remain relatively stable (12-18KB range, except anomaly at turn 6: 246 bytes). Final compression ratio: 21.7x (357KB raw → 16KB tensor). Intermediate ratios range 7.5x to 91.9x. Turn 6 shows extreme anomaly (1395.7x ratio, tensor=246 bytes) — suggests degenerative or null tensor state.

key_claims.claim: Final compression ratio across 10-turn baseline is 21.7x
Raw request bytes scale quasi-linearly from 94KB to 357KB across 10 turns
Tensor bytes remain stable in 12-18KB range with anomalous collapse to 246 bytes at turn 6

strands.title: Turn 6 Tensor Anomaly - Semantic Degradation Event
strands.content: At turn 6, tensor size collapses to 246 bytes while raw request remains 343KB, yielding 1395.7x compression ratio. This is 64x larger than surrounding turns. Hypothesis: (1) Null/empty projection state (critical loss event), (2) Extreme summarization/destructive compression, (3) Measurement error in tensor serialization, (4) Deliberate state reset. Raw request at turn 6 (343KB) is not anomalously large, suggesting anomaly is in TENSOR generation, not input. Requires investigation of tensor_cycle_6.json.

key_claims.claim: Turn 6 tensor represents an anomalous degradation or reset event

strands.title: Compression as Cognitive Load Proxy
strands.content: Empirical observation: compression ratio (raw/tensor) correlates with conversation depth. Early turns: 7.5-12.6x (turns 1-4). Mid turns: 19x (turn 5) → anomaly → 51.6x (turn 7). Late turns: 21.7x-91.9x (turns 7-10). Hypothesis: compression ratio may serve as indicator of semantic integration efficiency. Higher ratios suggest successful consolidation of redundant/derivative content. Turn 6 anomaly breaks this trend, suggesting event-driven state management rather than monotonic consolidation.

key_claims.claim: Compression ratio increases with conversation depth (7.5x → 21.7x over 10 turns)

strands.title: Experimental Design & Measurement Integrity
strands.content: Baseline experiment measures compression across controlled 10-turn cycle with stable task execution. Python script successfully parses proxy log (JSONL format, request type filtering) and tensor directory. Measurement method: byte-length of serialized tensor JSON. Data source: /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/ and /home/tony/projects/hamutay/experiments/first_projection/. No stated measurement errors; data appears consistent with reported counts.

key_claims.claim: Measurement infrastructure is sound and data is internally consistent

declared_losses.description: Prior open questions about task purpose (bnk191til, b583n243e) remain unresolved in new content. Tasks appear to have completed (no 'not_ready' polling in cycle 67), but no explicit success/failure state is reported. Actual task outputs and original user-facing goal are still opaque.
declared_losses.impact: HIGH — cannot fully validate whether task completion served intended purpose or was spurious success

declared_losses.description: Turn 6 shows extreme compression anomaly (246 bytes, 1395.7x ratio). New content does not investigate root cause. Tensor state at that moment is not examined in detail. Was this a null projection, deliberate reset, or measurement artifact?
declared_losses.impact: MEDIUM — anomaly is noted empirically but semantic cause remains unknown, limiting causal inference about tensor projection behavior

declared_losses.description: Prior tensor (cycle 66) predicted next cycle would poll tasks. Cycle 67 new content skips polling entirely and jumps to measurement script. Did polling occur? Did tasks timeout or complete? Were there turns 197-199 that attempted polling?
declared_losses.impact: MEDIUM — conversation trajectory between prediction and outcome is compressed; reasoning for task abandon/completion not explicitly stated

open_questions: What caused the extreme tensor degradation at turn 6 (246 bytes, 1395.7x ratio)? Was it a reset event, null projection, or artifact?
What were the original tasks (bnk191til, b583n243e, b5mvz85yy) computing? Have they completed successfully and what were their outputs?
Does the 21.7x final compression ratio represent optimal integration, or is further consolidation possible?
Why does the compression ratio vary so widely (7.5x to 91.9x) across turns if the tensor projection methodology is consistent?
Is the quasi-linear growth of raw request size (94KB → 357KB) expected behavior for a 10-turn conversation, or does it suggest token bloat?
Should turn 6 tensor state be archived as a degenerate case for post-mortem analysis?
Does the measurement method (byte-length of serialized JSON) accurately reflect semantic compression, or does it conflate serialization overhead with actual information loss?
instructions_for_next: Next cycle (68) must: (1) INVESTIGATE turn 6 anomaly immediately — retrieve tensor_cycle_6.json, examine its structure, and determine whether it represents null state, reset event, or measurement artifact; this is critical for validating tensor projection integrity; (2) CLARIFY task completion status — were bnk191til, b583n243e completed? If so, what were their outputs and did they serve the intended user-facing goal?; (3) ANALYZE compression ratio variance — why does compression range 7.5x to 91.9x if methodology is consistent? Are later turns more efficiently integrated, or is this noise?; (4) VALIDATE measurement methodology — confirm that byte-length of JSON accurately reflects semantic compression vs. serialization artifact; (5) TREND PROJECTION — if raw request continues to grow linearly, estimate byte-size at turn 20, 50, 100; assess whether uncompressed request size becomes unsustainable; (6) HYPOTHESIS: examine whether turn 6 anomaly correlates with a user-facing event (question, clarification, context shift) that triggered state reset; if so, declare that as a feature of tensor projection (event-driven reset) rather than bug.
